FT_IC1.evidence: Uses U.S. ABCD Study baseline through 2-year follow-up (2016-2018, ages 9-10 years)

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical analysis with regression models examining associations between cumulative ACEs, bullying victimization, and mental health outcomes

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed published paper (Journal of Youth and Adolescence, 2024)

FT_IC2.evidence: Paper examines ACEs and bullying victimization (including cyberbullying) in relation to internalizing/externalizing problems. However, cyberbullying/bullying is measured as a single construct without a separately-measured smartphone or social media use variable. Per v6 criteria, cyberbullying alone without separately-measured SM/phone variable does NOT qualify. No qualifying smartphone or SM exposure variable paired with individual-trait outcome.

notes: Excluded under FT-EC2: no qualifying smartphone/SM variable paired with individual-trait-level outcome.

FT_IC1.evidence: U.S. ABCD Study, children enrolled ages 9-10 years (baseline 2016-2018) from 21 U.S. sites, followed annually for up to 4 years

FT_IC5.evidence: Original empirical longitudinal analysis using linear mixed models to examine associations between digital media use and ADHD-related symptoms across 4 years of follow-up

FT_IC6.evidence: Peer-reviewed published paper (Pediatrics Open Science, Volume 2, Issue 1, 2026)

FT_IC2.evidence: Social media use qualifies as IV (measured via Youth Screen Time Survey: time on Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, etc.). Individual-trait-level DV includes ADHD-related symptoms (inattention, hyperactivity-impulsivity) measured via parent-reported Child Behavior Checklist. Relationship pairs SM variable with individual-trait mental health/behavioral outcome.

FT_IC3.evidence: Multiple numeric results reported: longitudinal association social media × time on inattention symptoms β [SE] = 0.03 [0.01], P < .001; cumulative 4-year effect β = 0.15 (SE, 0.03), P < .001. Cross-lagged panel models confirm unidirectional association β [SE] = 0.03 [0.01], P = .004.

FT_IC4.evidence: Numeric quantitative results present throughout: standardized β coefficients with standard errors and p-values for SM use predicting ADHD symptoms; effect sizes reported; results robust across sensitivity and additional analyses

FT_EC7_reason: Design is longitudinal (baseline ages 9-10, followed 4 years) but social media is NOT DV-only. Social media use appears as IV predicting inattention symptom change over time. Condition 2 (SM as DV in every analysis) not met because SM serves IV role in primary analyses.

FT_IC1.evidence: Abstract explicitly references "Adolescent Brain Cognitive Development (ABCD) study" with 2-year follow-up data; default rule applies given mention of ABCD study name

FT_IC5.evidence: No PDF available; abstract snippet is minimal and does not clearly establish whether this is original empirical analysis or review/commentary. Title refers to "predictors of depression" but insufficient detail to confirm empirical original analysis status.

notes: No PDF. Abstract insufficient to determine FT-IC5 status (whether original empirical vs. review). Cascade deferred at FT-IC5. Classified as NA_FOR_NOW per v6 protocol.
